<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
@ph-kev wrote This PR (https://github.com/JuliaGeo/CFTime.jl/pull/69) in CFTime broke this snippet of code in ClimaUtilities (https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaUtilities.jl/blob/34cb6fd78150e01f2ce1b5427f3da1a96b273561/ext/nc_common.jl#L14-L20).

There was a separate bug related to the latest CTS so this also restricts to CTS < 0.8.10
## To-do
remove CFTime dep and compat once ClimaUtilities is updated and released
remove CTS compat restriction once the bug is fixed


## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->


<!---

title: Add CFTime dep and compat to avoid bug in ClimaUtilities

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Our current bare soil evaporation scheme doesn't have any calibration parameters. It is also annoying to implement fully correctly since it requires knowing the potential evaporation rate, which in principal should be a separate call to surface fluxes (to get the conductance for a saturated surface - which we don't do right now)  This changes the scheme so that we have two free parameters to calibrate. It also (with default parameters) reduces the biases as:
H: 0.9 -> -0.7
LE: -2 -> -0.2
SW_u: 1.2 -> 1.7 # increase
LW_u -> 1.1 -> 0.5
with minimal impact to RMSE:
H: 11.3 -> 11.5
LE: 11.5 -> 11.6
SW_u: 9.5 -> 9.6
LW_u: 5.3 -> 5.2 # decrease
based on the 2008 global run forced by ERA5.


I can also try calibrating these parameters if preferred before we merge this

## To-do



## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->

title: Test different bare soil evaporation scheme with calibratable parameters
